title: Juice on Carpet
stainChemistry: Fruit juice contains anthocyanins or carotenoids (depending on fruit), citric acid, fructose, and tannins. On carpet, the pigment bonds through hydrogen bonding, and the sugar component creates a sticky matrix that traps the dye against the fiber. Oxidation over time deepens the color and makes the stain increasingly resistant to treatment.
whyThisWorks: The acidic tannin spotter breaks pigment-fiber bonds on carpet. Hydrogen peroxide oxidizes the chromophore structures responsible for color. Quick treatment is key — fresh juice stains are water-soluble, but dried stains require significantly more effort as the sugars crystallize and tannins cross-link.
defaultAssumption: Assume stain age is unknown and possibly heat-set unless user specifies otherwise. Start conservative.

steps: 1. Apply Tannin Formula (acidic tannin spotter) and tamp gently. Work from edge to center — 5-10 min dwell time
2. Apply H2O2 6% (professional oxidizer) to break down color-causing bonds. Monitor every 5 min — 5-15 min dwell time
3. Flush with NSD (Neutral Synthetic Detergent) and leveling agent to remove broken-down tannin — 2-3 min dwell time
4. Apply Acetic Acid 28% (diluted 1:10) as neutralizing rinse — 1-2 min dwell time
warnings: NEVER use ammonia or alkaline products on tannin stains — permanently darkens the color
Avoid over-wetting — extract excess moisture to prevent backing damage

steps: 1. Blot immediately with a clean white cloth to absorb as much liquid as possible — never rub
2. Mix 1 tbsp Dawn dish soap with 1 cup hydrogen peroxide 3%. Apply to stain — 5-10 min dwell time
3. Blot with clean white cloth. Repeat until color fades
4. Rinse with cold water to remove all cleaning solution
5. Air dry — do NOT use dryer until stain is completely gone
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if the stain hasn't improved after 3 applications, or if you see any color change in the fabric. Take to a professional dry cleaner
warnings: NEVER use baking soda, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners on tannin stains — they permanently darken the color
Avoid heat until stain is fully removed

neverDo: No chlorine bleach on colored carpet
Avoid over-wetting — causes backing damage and mold
fiberSensitivities: Most synthetic carpet (nylon/polyester) tolerates standard chemistry
Always blot from outside in to prevent spreading
Extract excess moisture to prevent backing damage

escalation.when: If juice stain persists after 2-3 professional treatment cycles, or if the stain has been heat-set, or if home treatment has not improved the stain after 3 attempts
escalation.whatToTell: Explain that the stain is juice on carpet, how old it is, and what products were already used. This helps the professional choose the right chemistry without duplicating failed approaches
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaning service with truck-mount extraction

contextQuestions: Has it been exposed to heat (dryer, iron, hot water)?
Have you already tried treating it? If so, with what products?
Has it been exposed to any alkaline products (baking soda, ammonia)?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Stain age and heat exposure are the two biggest factors. Fresh stains with no heat exposure have the highest success rate. Heat-set or aged stains may require professional treatment.
